title: Psychotria aetantha
intro: Psychotria aetantha is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the "Brazilian guava" or "Maracuja branco". The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include the potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, suggesting its use in treating various health conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Psychotria aetantha include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Psychotria aetantha is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in topical creams and salves to support wound healing and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Psychotria aetantha is a species within the genus Psychotria, which belongs to the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The Rubiaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, comprising over 600 genera and more than 13,000 species. Psychotria aetantha is native to the tropical regions of the Americas, specifically in Central and South America. The classification of Psychotria aetantha is further supported by its distinct morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic analysis. The precise classification and taxonomic status of Psychotria aetantha continue to be the subject of ongoing research and study.
